#Overview

**The Challenge:**
Build a predictive model that city officials would actually want to use, using only the techniques we’ve covered this semester.

**The Stakes:**
Your presentation will be evaluated by a panel of judges (your professor + a surprise guest) who will grill you on your methods, assumptions, and recommendations. You need to demonstrate not just technical competence, but deep understanding of what you’ve built and why it matters.

## Phase 1: Data Cleaning

#### Step 1: Load Libraries


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
#Clearing previous data
rm(list=ls())

library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tidycensus)
library(tigris)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)
```
:::


#### Step 2: Load & Inspect Bus Ridership


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
bus_rider_2022 <- st_read("Data/Fall_2022_Stop_Summary_(Bus).csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  select(-source,-gis_db_id) 
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Reading layer `Fall_2022_Stop_Summary_(Bus)' from data source 
  `/Users/indrajeetchavan/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-PennO365/Penn/7. Penn Sem 2/CPLN 5920 PPA/GitHub/IC_PPARep/Labs/PPA_Finals/Lab_6/Data/Fall_2022_Stop_Summary_(Bus).csv' 
  using driver `CSV'
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
colnames(bus_rider_2022) <- c ("x", "y", "fid", "mode", "route", "direction", "route_1", "stop",
  "lat", "lon", "season", "week_on", "week_off", "sat_on", "sat_off",
  "sun_on", "sun_off")

bus_rider_2023 <- st_read("Data/Fall_2023_Stop_Summary_(Bus).csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  select(-gisdbid)
```

  "sun_on", "sun_off")

bus_rider <- bind_rows(bus_rider_2022, bus_rider_2023, bus_rider_2024, bus_rider_2025)

:::


#### Step 3: Filtering Bus Ridership Data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Load Philadelphia boundary
PhillyBoundary <- 
  st_read("Data/Census_Tracts_2010-shp/57b9fc6a-ab88-44db-8773-2a46a97483252020328-1-1vm5ejc.1re.shp") %>%
  st_set_crs(2272)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Reading layer `57b9fc6a-ab88-44db-8773-2a46a97483252020328-1-1vm5ejc.1re' from data source `/Users/indrajeetchavan/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-PennO365/Penn/7. Penn Sem 2/CPLN 5920 PPA/GitHub/IC_PPARep/Labs/PPA_Finals/Lab_6/Data/Census_Tracts_2010-shp/57b9fc6a-ab88-44db-8773-2a46a97483252020328-1-1vm5ejc.1re.shp' 
  using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 384 features and 14 fields
Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: 2660575 ymin: 204813.9 xmax: 2750113 ymax: 304942.4
Projected CRS: NAD83 / Pennsylvania South (ftUS)
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)

bus_rider <- bus_rider %>%
  mutate(
    x = as.numeric(x),
    y = as.numeric(y)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(x), !is.na(y))

#Aggregating Data to each bus stop 
bus_rider_stops <- bus_rider %>%
  group_by(route_1)

# Convert to spatial (sf object)
bus_rider_sf <- bus_rider %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(2272)

# Write to GeoJSON
st_write(
  st_transform(bus_rider_sf, 4326),
  "bus_rider.geojson",
  driver = "GeoJSON",
  delete_dsn = TRUE
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Deleting source `bus_rider.geojson' using driver `GeoJSON'
Writing layer `bus_rider' to data source `bus_rider.geojson' using driver `GeoJSON'
Writing 72685 features with 15 fields and geometry type Point.
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Map
p1 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(data = PhillyBoundary, fill = "gray95", color = "gray60") +
  geom_sf(data = bus_rider_sf, color = "#d62828", size = 0.1, alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(
    title = "Bus Stop Locations",
    subtitle = paste0("Bus Stops in Philly, n = ", nrow(bus_rider))
  )

p1
```
